name: Architext 1.6.3 Server-Side Plan Precompute
summary: Dense diagrams render instantly: architext serve precomputes every flow x view route plan in Node (where planning is ~20x faster than the browser) under strict input-key parity, and the viewer fetches finished plans with full in-browser fallback. Routing performance is now gated by a ratchet whose baseline only moves toward improvement, slow plans persist their per-phase timing breakdowns, winner selection is 1.27x faster with byte-identical routes, and the README/public face refresh is a release rule.
updateSource: 1.6.3 RC: all scope merged to main (PRs #105, #107, #108, #109, #110); awaiting GitHub release + trusted publish.

required.title: Server-side plan precompute, viewer fetch-first
required.summary: architext serve precomputes every flow x compatible-flows-view plan in a worker thread at startup, after every settled+validated data change, and after every config write. Plans are stored under sha256(planInputKey) — the canonical hash of the exact planner input, built by the shared planRequest module both sides import — and served via GET /api/plan/<sha>. The viewer renders a precomputed plan only on an exact key match and otherwise plans in-browser unchanged: the cache can miss, it cannot lie. The 18-step dense flow that took 2.5 minutes in Chrome renders from one fetch.

required.title: Persisted plan-timing breakdowns
required.summary: Slow plans (those that showed the loading overlay) persist their full per-phase timing breakdown: a structured console record plus a localStorage ring buffer (architext.planTimings, last 20), and timing on the hook state — so a long plan is reportable after the overlay is gone instead of a stopwatch exercise.

required.title: One-pass route winner selection
required.summary: CPU profiling put sortedRouteCandidates at 13.4% self-time with every caller consuming only the winner; replaced copy+sort with a single-pass argmin under the same total order. Corpus planning 45.8s -> 36.2s (1.27x), dense flow 6.7s -> ~5.2s, all 29 corpus plans byte-identical via the new route-parity fingerprint harness (shipped in viewer/tools).

required.title: Performance ratchet test gate
required.summary: Testing includes a performance threshold baselined on achieved results that only moves toward improvement: deterministic work counters gate exactly per corpus flow (zero CI flake), a machine-normalized wall ratio with interleaved calibration catches gross slowdowns under any load, and regen-perf-baseline.mjs refuses upward movement (median of three cold child-process runs).

required.title: README screenshots refresh + release rule
required.summary: All README screenshots re-captured at the current UI (they dated from 1.4.x), Repo Tree and Blast Radius added, the capture script hardened (readiness selectors, overlay wait, runs the plan farm), and a high-criticality Release rule added: every release re-runs the capture and updates the roboticus-site card — a release is not complete while the public face shows a prior version.

required.title: Roadmap: parallelize the plan farm
required.summary: Planned roadmap item recorded: fan the precompute farm across a worker-thread pool (plans are independent), warming default view x flow pairs first; maps onto goroutine fan-out in the 1.7.0 Go backend.

workstreams.name: Plan performance
workstreams.summary: Server-side precompute under strict key parity, persisted timing telemetry, argmin winner selection, and the only-improves performance ratchet.

workstreams.name: Release hygiene
workstreams.summary: Public-face refresh with enforcement rule, and roadmap bookkeeping.
